rulings.Case Title: CONS. OF MABLE LEE FERNANDEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #28, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/16/2020 (no earlier date available). 1) No proposed orders have been received. Please mail two copies to Room 202. 2) No courtesy copy of the account statements filed on 10/29/2020 has been received. Please mail it to Room 202. 3) Paragraph 4 of the petition: Is the current bond amount accurate? The Court's record shows an additional bond of $636,000 filed after the sale confirmation hearing. 4) Paragraph 10: The attorney's invoices are attached to the petition, but are not marked as Exhibit 2. 5) Paragraph 11: Schedule C shows payment to the court-appointed counsel. 6) Paragraph 12 (and paragraph 5 of the prayer for relief): A specific description of each asset proposed to be distributed must be alleged. If cash, the net amount as of a date certain must be alleged. 7) Schedule D: If the furniture and furnishings were valueless, why did the petitioner report in the prior account that their fair market value was $7,000? 8) Did the petitioner comply with CRC 7.1059(b)(17) before disposing of the furniture/furnishings and car? 9) Exhibit 1: i) Please provide the sub-total hours spent by the petitioner each month. ii) Between 11/13/2018 and 11/27/2018, why did the petitioner need to spend more than 24 hours preparing the prior account? iii) Should the petitioner be compensated from the estate for the time spent on the research of reimbursed expenses (7.6 hours between 02/15/2019 and 02/20/2019)? iv) It appears that the petitioner started to spend less time monthly (0.6 hours) on "mail review, bill approval, and file maintenance" after 03/31/2019, but there was a one-time increase (1.0 hours) on 08/29/2019. Please explain why. v) What is the "trust document" the petitioner sent to the CPA on 02/20/2020? 10) Re the attorney's invoices attached: i) Why did the attorney and paralegal need to spend more than 7 hours to respond to the examiner's notes between 02/11/2019 and 03/04/2019? ii) Should the attorney be compensated from the estate for the time spent on the "file management for court hearing" on 08/28/2020? iii) Should the attorney be compensated from the estate for the time spent on internal communications between the attorney and paralegal on 08/29/2020 and 08/31/2020? iv) Will the attorney refund to the estate an unearned portion of the $2,000 requested as anticipated compensation to close this matter?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CYNTHIA MAUREEN BELLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/16/20. The administrator must supplement her petition to address the following issues and to attach supporting documents where appropriate: The I&A consisted of retirement policies all listed on Attachment 1, but it is unclear if they were, in fact, cash or cash equivalents; the administrator's costs include funeral expenses for which she did not file a creditor's claim [Probate Code Section 9000(a)(3)] and for which none of the payees is identified; other costs for File&ServeXpress are not itemized to separate filing fees from the cost of filing (when most E-filing costs are just $5 per E-filed document); the administrator's costs include $2,290.75 for "Will Contest Attorney's Fees" which appear to be extraordinary services (the attorney payee is not identified), was not approved by the Court, and do not appear to have been for the benefit of the estate; other costs paid to ReCares ($100) and the United Negro College Fund ($500) have no description. A courtesy copy of the verified supplement (and any other papers filed to address these issues) must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 12/7/20.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ERNEST LUBBE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Hearing issues have been e-mailed to counsel. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 7th Amended General Order which took effect on 10/30/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KEITH H. HOPKINSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/16/2020 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) The date of issuance of letters set forth in the introductory paragraph of the petition is incorrect. 2) The date and county of death set forth in paragraph 2 of the petition are incorrect. 3) Paragraph 8: Did all the IRAs listed on the inventory and appraisal have designated beneficiaries other than this estate? If so, a corrected inventory and appraisal removing the IRAs must be filed. The IRAs must also be removed from the statutory fee base. 4) Paragraph 11: No waiver signed by Edwin Dyer has been filed. Please provide a copy of the waiver. 5) Paragraph 13: A detailed schedule of gains must be filed. CRC 7.550(b)(6). 6) Paragraph 12: Notice to the California Victim Compensation Board is required if any heir or beneficiary is or has previously been confined. See Probate Code section 9202(b). 7) The attorney's declaration (filed on 10/14/2020) shows notice given to the Franchise Tax Board contained an incorrect case number. Also, the notice was not given on a Judicial Council form DE-157. 8) The petition must list specific descriptions of all assets of this estate proposed to be distributed and identify who should receive which asset. CRC 7.651 & LRSF 14.67(L)(7)(a). 9) No order for final distribution from the predeceased spouse's estate has been issued in the related case (PES-19-302553). Is this estate ready to be closed before receiving the distribution from the predeceased spouse's estate? 10) Descriptions of the estate's distributable assets on hand and identities of distributees must be set forth in the body of a proposed order (no attachment is allowed). LRSF 14.67(L)(7)(b).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL FRANCIS MCGOVERN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/16/20. Now yet another (second) amended petition has been filed, for which notice must be mailed and published. Notice may not be mailed or published before the pleading requiring notice is filed. The second amended petition was just filed on 11/10/20, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. The mailed and published notice must clearly indicate that an AMENDED (or, in this case, a Second Amended) petition has been filed. The "Amended Petition" filed on 11/10/20 is incomplete: no copy of the will is attached. Neither the caption nor item 2c of the petition indicates that IAEA authority is being requested. No Proof of Subscribing Witness has been filed yet. The petitioner (the person named in item 2 of the petition) is still missing from item 8 of the petition. Item 8 of the petition still does not indicate who the parents were/are and when they died.

rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Hearing on status of closing estate will be set for 03/09/2022. Appearance required if petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report of status of administration is not on file by 03/02/2022.
